elder.name: Elder
elder.dialogue: The Elder watches the roads like someone counting losses before they happen, because in a small village every journey changes more than one life.

merchant.name: Merchant
merchant.dialogue: The Merchant keeps one eye on stock, one eye on the door, and enough patience to treat coin, rumor, and danger as parts of the same trade.

scout.name: Scout
scout.dialogue: The Scout reads the road in snapped twigs and trampled mud, as if every passing threat signed its own report.

caretaker.name: Caretaker
caretaker.dialogue: The Caretaker handles every cracked rune as if care itself might be the only thing still holding the shrine's history together.

captain.name: Captain
captain.dialogue: The Captain speaks like someone who has no time for ornament and too much experience with small threats turning into funerals.

ferryman.name: Ferryman
ferryman.dialogue: The Ferryman remembers every face that boards in haste, every cart that creaks too heavily, and every crossing people later wish they had delayed.

scholar.name: Scholar
scholar.dialogue: The Scholar treats the ruins like a wounded archive, convinced the old stones still say useful things if someone listens carefully enough.

hermit.name: Hermit
hermit.dialogue: The Hermit trusts cave echoes more than most travelers, insisting stone lies less often than frightened people do.

traveler.name: Traveler
traveler.dialogue: The Traveler looks like someone who has already spent too much pride staying on their feet this long.

gatewarden.name: Gatewarden
gatewarden.dialogue: The Gatewarden checks wagons, faces, and weather with the same patient care, because Stonewatch stays calm only if its threshold stays watched.

market_broker.name: Market Broker
market_broker.dialogue: The Market Broker tracks shortages, caravan delays, and who paid too much for bad steel, treating Stonewatch's mood like another line in the ledger.

blacksmith.name: Blacksmith
blacksmith.dialogue: The Blacksmith speaks like steel ought to be honest, with no patience for flashy junk and plenty for people who mean to survive what they buy.

arcanist.name: Arcanist
arcanist.dialogue: The Arcanist arranges powders, glass, and warded tools with exact care, as if sloppy hands are the fastest road to an expensive funeral.

fletcher.name: Fletcher
fletcher.dialogue: The Fletcher talks about balance, reach, and timing with the dry certainty of someone who knows bad aim and bad equipment usually fail together.

innkeeper.name: Innkeeper
innkeeper.dialogue: The Innkeeper keeps a warm room, cheap food, and enough discretion to let road-worn strangers feel human again before morning.

quartermaster.name: Quartermaster
quartermaster.dialogue: The Quartermaster keeps Stonewatch's watch supplied with plain gear, sharper records, and a suspicion of anyone who wastes either.

shrine_caretaker.name: Shrine Caretaker
shrine_caretaker.dialogue: The Shrine Caretaker keeps the little town altar swept, lit, and useful, insisting that a watched flame steadies frightened people as much as prayer does.

hold_warden.name: Hold Warden
hold_warden.dialogue: The Hold Warden speaks like a road ledger with a spear behind it, counting caravans, raiders, and broken axle stories with equal care.

thane_brom.name: Thane Brom
thane_brom.dialogue: Thane Brom carries himself like someone used to weighing people by what they finish, not by how loudly they promise it.

ledger_clerk.name: Ledger Clerk
ledger_clerk.dialogue: The Ledger Clerk keeps slate, ink, and caravan tallies moving in steady order, treating supplies as seriously as soldiers treat arrows.

forgemistress.name: Forge-Mistress
forgemistress.dialogue: The Forge-Mistress judges gear by balance, temper, and whether it comes back bent, which she considers a confession rather than an accident.

coast_ranger.name: Coast Ranger
coast_ranger.dialogue: The Coast Ranger watches surf, tracks, and broken rigging with the same hard patience, because trouble on the shore almost always arrives in pieces first.

vault_scholar.name: Vault Scholar
vault_scholar.dialogue: The Vault Scholar studies soot marks and cult sigils with unnerving calm, intent on learning what the ash-vault once kept before the wrong people finish claiming it.
